items.title: X users report platform glitch, say video sharing impossible
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: X (formerly Twitter) users have reported problems with the platform's video link copy feature since March 31, with many saying it no longer works. According to International Business Times, users attempting to share video clips, including sports highlights and viral content, said videos now auto-play instead of generating direct links. The issue has sparked frustration, with users saying it is harder to share content or embed videos on other platforms.

items.title: Pakistan says onus on Afghanistan to end hostilities
items.summary: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan said Thursday that the burden of ending its conflict with Afghanistan lay with Kabul, as the two sides held preliminary talks to try to end hostilities. Negotiations started in Urumqi, in northwest China, on Wednesday, after Pakistan's Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar travelled to Beijing for talks with his Chinese counterpart, Wang Yi. Pakistan foreign ministry spokesman Tahir Hussain Andrabi told reporters in the capital that the government hoped for a "durable solution."

items.title: Motorcyclist's leg severed, another seriously injured in Jalan Sultan Ismail crash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Two motorcyclists were seriously injured, including one whose leg was severed, in a crash with a car at a traffic light junction along Jalan Sultan Ismail here early today. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department (TEID) chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the incident, which involved a Honda City and two Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycles, occurred at 4.27am. The 24-year-old victim suffered a severed left leg, while the 19-year-old sustained fractures to his left arm and left leg.

items.title: Energy crisis: Energy use in govt buildings tightened
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Energy controls in government buildings will be tightened, including setting air-conditioning temperatures at no lower than 24 degrees Celsius, following the global energy crisis, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. "As a proactive response to the global energy crisis, the government will implement several energy-saving measures at the national level," he said at the Global Energy Crisis Briefing, broadcast live by local television stations today. The ongoing global energy crisis, following conflict in West Asia, has had a direct impact on energy supply and prices worldwide," he said.

items.title: Akmal: Government adopts proactive measures to tackle global energy crisis
items.summary: NILAI: The government is taking a proactive approach in assessing the global energy crisis, focusing on medium-term planning to ensure Malaysia's economic stability remains strong and protected. Economy Minister, Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir, said the ministry expects the conflict to last at least six months, but initial plans have been drawn up to deal with any eventuality. "The ministry expects that the conflict will last six months at the earliest.

items.title: Toxic beauty: MOH bans two cosmetic products over mercury and other poisons
items.summary: It said in a statement today that the two products contained prohibited substances such as mercury, hydroquinone, tretinoin and betamethasone 17-valerate. “Mercury can also cause rashes, irritation and other changes to the skin,” said the statement. The MOH said the use of cosmetics containing these substances without professional health supervision can cause various side effects.

items.title: Malaysia well-positioned amid global headwinds: Bank Negara deputy governor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The country is well-positioned to weather an increasingly challenging global economic landscape but must move decisively to seize opportunities arising from ongoing structural shifts, said Bank Negara Malaysia deputy governor Datuk Marzunisham Omar. Malaysia's strong economic fundamentals, underpinned by resilient domestic demand, low inflation and a robust external position, continue to provide a key buffer against heightened global uncertainties, he added. In the current context, he said the uncertain global environment requires Malaysia to enhance its adaptability, particularly in addressing supply chain disruptions and shifts in global trade patterns.

items.title: Adopt pragmatic approach to tourism subsidy
items.summary: LETTER: The Transport Ministry needs to take serious concern over the rising cost of tourism transportation following the exclusion of tour buses from subsidies under the Subsidised Diesel Control Scheme (SKDS 2.0). The tourism industry remains one of the key drivers of the national economy. Without support for tourism transportation, costs will continue to rise.

items.title: Proton expects to hit RM30bil in vendor spend by 2030
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Proton Holdings Bhd expects to continue supporting its vendor ecosystem with procurement projected to reach RM30 billion over the next five years, reinforcing its role in strengthening Malaysia's automotive industry. Proton said it remains focused on ensuring the local automotive sector stays relevant and competitive amid rapid technological shifts, in line with the government's broader mobility agenda. "Looking ahead, Proton remains committed to supporting the continued growth and development of Malaysia's automotive industry," it said.

items.title: Anwar urges legal profession to rethink role in AI era
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on the legal profession to undergo a fundamental shift in values, skills and knowledge to remain relevant in the era of artificial intelligence (AI) and rapid global change. Speaking at the inaugural Commonwealth Legal Education Association (CLEA) Conference, he said the future of lawyering must go beyond mastering statutes and precedents, as these are increasingly becoming the domain of advanced AI systems. He said law students must be trained to act as ethical filters in an increasingly complex data environment.

items.title: Power outage stalls Ampang/Sri Petaling line, Rapid Rail rolls out shuttle buses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Train services on the Ampang/Sri Petaling route are experiencing delays due to a power outage between Awan Besar Station and Sri Petaling Station. For alternative train services, trains from Putra Heights Station will turn back at Kinrara BK5 Station with an expected frequency of seven minutes, while trains from Sentul Timur Station will turn back at Bandar Tasik Selatan Station with a frequency of 10 minutes. In addition, shuttle trains are provided between Bandar Tasik Selatan Station and Awan Besar Station and between Kinrara BK5 Station and Awan Besar Station with an expected frequency of 15 minutes.

items.title: VTAREC: Focus on risky behaviour, not just drink-driving, to cut road deaths
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Road fatalities in Malaysia are driven largely by human error, risky behaviour and systemic safety gaps rather than alcohol-impaired driving alone, according to the Vehicle Theft and Accident Reduction Council of Malaysia (VTAREC). Its coordinator, Mas Tina Abdul Hamid, said the council recently compiled a report showing that while drunk driving often dominates public discourse, it represents only a small portion of overall road deaths. "Drink-driving represents only a small fraction of Malaysia's road fatalities.

items.title: Rider’s leg severed in early morning KL crash, Honda City driver arrested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — A 24-year-old motorcyclist had his leg severed in a horrific crash after a car allegedly ran a red light along Jalan Sultan Ismail here early this morning. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Zamzuri Isa said the collision occurred at about 4.27am, Free Malaysia Today reported.. Both victims were rushed to Kuala Lumpur Hospital for treatment.

items.title: Heat check: Environment Ministry to brief Cabinet on dry spell, cloud seeding in play
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES) plans to submit a detailed weather forecast report on the current dry and hot weather to the Cabinet next week. Minister of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said the move was to enable the Cabinet to discuss if there was an urgent need to intervene, including cloud seeding operations. “However, NRES will always provide forecast information from time to time,” he said when commenting on NRES’s latest preparations in dealing with hot weather.

items.title: Police nab man over series of gold shop thefts, RM50k bracelets seized
items.summary: PENAMPANG: A man suspected of involvement in a series of thefts at jewellery shops around Penampang and Putatan was arrested by police on Tuesday. The 45-year-old local suspect was detained by a team from the Penampang district police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Division at 1pm at a commercial building in Kota Kinabalu. Penampang district police chief, Superintendent Sammy Newton, said further investigations led to the seizure of six gold bracelets of various sizes.

items.title: Fix rice supply, not just stockpile, says researcher
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Malaysia must move beyond expanding rice stockpiles and address structural weaknesses in its food system as global disruptions intensify. Legislative researcher John Ooh Sin Hwa said raising reserves to nine months offered only short-term relief. Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu recently announced an increase in stockpiles from six to nine months.

items.title: Sabah Tourism Board: Preparedness is key, destination remains compelling despite global jitters
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — The Sabah Tourism Board (STB) is establishing a crisis response unit to strengthen preparedness and ensure swift, coordinated action in the face of global uncertainties affecting the tourism sector. Assistant Minister to the Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Joniston Bangkuai said the move reflects a proactive approach to managing risks arising from geopolitical developments and economic challenges, while maintaining confidence in Sabah as a destination. Meanwhile, STB Chief Executive Officer Julinus Jeffery Jimit said about 60 per cent of Sabah’s visitor arrivals are domestic, with China remaining the main international market.

items.title: eSports star Ryzz scores 9As in SPM [WATCH]
items.summary: The fifth of eight siblings, the student from SMK Seksyen 7 Shah Alam said he began focusing seriously on achieving excellent SPM results last year during Form Four. "Since last year, I started to really focus on getting excellent results. "I made sure to manage my time properly for SPM.

items.title: Peer confrontation likely triggered Zara Qairina's distress before death, inquest hears
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The late Zara Qairina Mahathir might have been deprived of her usual coping mechanisms after a confrontation with a group of peers at SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha on July 15 last year. Expert witness Dr Chua Sze Hung testified that the 13-year-old's coping mechanisms largely involved seeking support and interaction with her peers, writing in diaries and, to some extent, non-suicidal self-injury. "On the night of the incident, after the confrontation at room A-3-6, I believe she was deprived of her usual coping mechanisms," he said before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the 63rd day of the inquest into her death.

items.title: ‘Justice delayed is justice denied’: Road Safety Council urges dedicated tribunals for crash victims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — The Road Safety Council has urged the government and the judiciary to consider establishing Special Road Accident Courts nationwide to expedite cases involving fatalities and serious injuries. Council member Lee Lam Thye said dedicated courts or tribunals would enable faster case resolution, ensure timely compensation and strengthen accountability among road users. “Justice delayed is justice denied,” he said in a statement, emphasising that prolonged legal processes deny victims timely closure and fair compensation.

items.title: Light at last for Hong Kong's Central office market after seven-year slump
items.summary: HONG KONG: Hong Kong's Grade-A office market in the city's financial core is finally hitting a floor after nearly seven years of decline, buoyed by a roaring capital market that is boosting demand from both mainland Chinese and multinational firms, realtors said. The turnaround follows a blockbuster IPO year in 2025, when funds raised in Hong Kong jumped 231 per cent to US$37 billion. "Companies are coming because of the capital markets, definitely," said Sam Gourlay, JLL Hong Kong head of office leasing advisory.

items.title: Bloomberg Malaysia seeks to strike out Bestinet libel suit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bloomberg (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd is seeking to have a libel suit struck out, arguing it had no role in publishing an article that linked Bestinet Sdn Bhd and its founder, Datuk Seri Aminul Islam Abdul Nor, to a migrant worker recruitment syndicate. In a High Court filing, Bloomberg (Malaysia), through lawyer Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad, described the plaintiffs' statement of claim dated Feb 12, as "scandalous, frivolous, and vexatious" and an abuse of the court process. The suit names 10 defendants, including Bloomberg LP, Bloomberg (Malaysia), The Edge Communications Sdn Bhd, Mnow Media Sdn Bhd, Mkini Dotcom Sdn Bhd, Norhafizan Muhamad and Nur Azwan Nusi of Harapan Media Enterprise, lawyer Latheefa Koya, Pandan member of parliament Datuk Seri Mohd Rafizi Ramli and activist Iswardy Morni.

items.title: War intensifies as Iran vows 'crushing' strikes on US and Israel
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran on Thursday threatened "crushing" attacks on the US and Israel, firing missiles at Tel Aviv after US President Donald Trump vowed to bomb the Islamic republic "back to the Stone Ages." In a prime-time White House address, Trump said the US was "very close" to achieving its objectives but warned attacks would intensify if Iran did not reach a negotiated settlement. Iran's response was immediate, with Israeli air defences pressed into action and police responding to "several" impact sites, as four people were reportedly lightly injured in the Tel Aviv area.

items.title: Penang Port Commission ramps up safeguards as global energy crisis rattles supply chains
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 2 — The Penang Port Commission (PPC) has stepped up efforts to safeguard supply chain continuity and ensure stable port operations amid the global energy crisis stemming from conflicts in West Asia and volatile oil prices. PPC chairman Datuk Yeoh Soon Hin said the current global situation has significant implications for logistics costs, energy price stability and overall supply chain resilience, placing pressure on ports and industrial activities worldwide. Additionally, Yeoh said the PPC has adopted prudent financial strategies, reinforcing budget discipline and cost control measures to ensure efficient resource allocation without compromising service standards.

items.title: Rule of law must reach the poorest, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today reaffirmed the government's commitment to strengthening access to justice, placing it at the core of ongoing legal and institutional reforms. He said access to justice has been made a central pillar of the government's broader reform agenda, particularly in addressing disparities faced by vulnerable groups. Also present were Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Minister Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh, and Universiti Malaya pro-chancellor Tan Sri Datuk Zainun Ali.

items.title: What is Malaysia Vision Valley 2.0? Inside Negeri Sembilan’s six-zone economic mega-project
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — You may have heard the name, but what exactly is Malaysia Vision Valley 2.0 (MVV 2.0)? Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir (left) and Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun speak to reporters during a visit to the construction site of the new highway linking Nilai, Labu, and Bandar Enstek in Nilai on April 2, 2026. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir (left) and Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun (centre) inspect plans during a site visit in Nilai on April 2, 2026.

items.title: Missing family of six found in Bentong
items.summary: KUANTAN: A woman and her five children, who were previously reported missing since Monday, were found safe after they came to the Bentong police headquarters last night. Bentong police chief Superintendent Zaiham Mohd Kahar said all involved arrived in safe condition around 9.30pm yesterday. "Preliminary investigations found that they left home due to family misunderstandings," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Witness statement strengthens conclusion on Zara's manner of death
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A witness statement from a student has strengthened a consultant forensic psychiatrist's conclusion on the manner of death of Zara Qairina Mahathir. Dr Chua Sze Hung, the 67th witness in Zara's inquest, was requested during proceedings to read the statement of the 65th child witness, believed to be the last person to have interacted with Zara on July 16. However, Dr Chua said the student was not among his interviewees.

items.title: Matrix Concepts spotlights MVV 2.0 vision, positions TechValley as key growth catalyst
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has unveiled the broader vision and investment potential of Malaysia Vision Valley City, its future integrated development within Malaysia Vision Valley 2.0 (MVV 2.0), with MVV TechValley as a key industrial driver. He added that MVV TechValley continues to attract strong interest due to its strategic connectivity and integration within a broader development vision. MVV TechValley is Matrix Concepts' second industrial development following the success of Sendayan TechValley, where all phases were fully taken up.

items.title: Director‑general and deputy of Environment Department expected in court over corruption case tied to e‑waste
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 2 — The director-general of the Department of Environment (DOE) and its deputy director-general are expected to be charged at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court tomorrow in connection with an investigation into electronic waste management. Confirming the matter, Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the charges would proceed following approval from the public prosecutor and the Attorney General. Azam had earlier said the MACC would intensify its efforts to combat corruption involving the management of e-waste and plastic waste, following developments implicating senior officers within the department.

items.title: Nation in crisis preparedness mode - Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the country is currently in crisis preparedness mode following global uncertainties which affect fuel prices. Therefore, Zahid who is also the rural and regional development minister, asked all parties not to point fingers at the government regarding the fuel price hike. He said the current situation was caused by the global crisis and required the cooperation of all parties to deal with it together.

items.title: Azam: MATF on right track after US$15.8bil recovery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's Multi-Agency Task Force (MATF) is on the right track in combating financial and corporate crimes, having recovered US$15.8 billion (RM64 billion) in illicit proceeds between 2023 and 2025. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said he hopes MATF will surpass the US$15.8 billion recovery figure within the next two years, stressing the need to further strengthen enforcement efforts. Azam, who is deputy chairman of MATF, said the achievement reflects an unprecedented level of cooperation among enforcement agencies in tackling offences such as tax evasion and corporate crime.

items.title: Cheah draws the line on RM11bil IJM deal: "Take it or we walk away" [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: With just days to go before the April 6 deadline, the Sunway Bhd founder and controlling shareholder Tan Sri Jeffrey Cheah has made his position clear: the RM11 billion takeover offer for IJM Corp Bhd is final. Cheah, who holds a 59.4 per cent stake in Sunway, said the group has already tabled its best and final offer. The group launched its takeover bid on Jan 12, offering RM3.15 each for IJM's 3.51 billion shares through a combination of cash and newly-issued Sunway shares, a move that could reshape Malaysia's construction and infrastructure landscape.

items.title: PM to visit Kuantan tomorrow
items.summary: KUANTAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to undertake a one-day working visit here tomorrow, focusing on district development initiatives as well as engagement with youth and silat practitioners. His political secretary, Datuk Ahmad Farhan Fauzi, said the visit will begin with Friday prayers at Masjid Saidina Umar Al-Khattab in Cenderawasih, followed by a meet-and-greet session with local residents. "After prayers, the prime minister will chair the Kuantan District Development Meeting at the District and Land Office to discuss local development priorities, including efforts to strengthen the district economy and improve public wellbeing," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Seven witnesses expected in Mindef drone trespass case
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Seven prosecution witnesses are expected to testify in the trial of two Chinese nationals accused of trespassing into a restricted zone at the Defence Ministry. On Aug 15 last year, then Wangsa Maju police chief Senior Assistant Commissioner Mohammad Lazim Ismail said the two were caught by Military Police while operating a drone on a football field near Wisma Pertahanan. The accused, photographers Li Luorong, 28, and Chen Junhao, 21, will stand trial after the Attorney-General's Chambers rejected their bid for a reduced charge.

items.title: Azam Baki confirms no MACC link in 'corporate mafia' probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Tan Sri Azam Baki has reiterated that police have found no involvement of Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) officers or its leadership in the ongoing investigation into alleged "corporate mafia" activities. The MACC chief commissioner said the matter has been fully handed over to the police and declined to comment further, stressing that he did not wish to interfere with ongoing investigations. It was previously reported that police investigations into the alleged "corporate mafia" case involving businessman Victor Chin have found no links to MACC personnel.

items.title: Intel looks to put millions more into SambaNova startup chaired by CEO Tan
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Chipmaker Intel has planned to invest another US$15 million in SambaNova, a chip startup chaired by Intel chief executive officer Lip-Bu Tan, a Reuters review of corporate records showed. The investment, subject to regulatory approval, would increase Intel's ownership of SambaNova to 9 per cent, the review showed. The backing follows another US$35 million that Intel put into SambaNova in February, which, along with other financing, had boosted Intel's stake in the startup to 8.2 per cent, from 6.8 per cent last year.

items.title: Global energy crisis: Ministry restructures programmes to shield rural communities
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Rural and Regional Development Ministry will restructure development programmes this year by prioritising critical projects to help rural communities cope with rising fuel and energy costs. Zahid, who is also rural and regional development minister, said the new approach will also focus on boosting income generation among small and medium entrepreneurs, particularly micro-entrepreneurs in rural areas, as part of efforts to address the rising cost of living. Earlier in his speech, Zahid said the ministry plays a strategic role as the frontline in building rural economic resilience.

items.title: Army camp assault: Superior allegedly slapped, punched soldiers over cleaning duties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The army has convened a board of inquiry into an incident at Kem Bentong that left a soldier in critical condition after he was allegedly assaulted by a Junior Non-Commissioned Officer (JNCO) on Monday. "The suspected JNCO has been detained by the police to assist in further investigations," it said. Yesterday, police detained an army corporal to assist in the investigations of the incident.

items.title: Zafrul: Govt needs standby stimulus ready to tackle sustained Middle East crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — The government must have a structured stimulus plan on standby, ready to be activated if the global energy crisis continues to exert pressure on Malaysia’s economy and the people’s cost of living, said former minister Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Aziz In a statement posted on social media, the former minister of investment, trade, and industry stressed that the current global conflict has created a “new normal” that will place sustained pressure on energy costs and supply chains for a prolonged period. He said that while additional measures may not be required today, the government must be prepared to act swiftly when circumstances change. “The next stimulus or additional initiative need not necessarily be large, but it must be structured, phased, and activated when necessary,” Tengku Zafrul wrote.

items.title: Johor police bust drug syndicate, seize narcotics worth RM1.2m
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 2 — Police have crippled a drug trafficking syndicate following two raids here on Monday and Tuesday, arresting six individuals and seizing various narcotics worth RM1.24 million. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the suspects — four local men, one Singaporean man, and one local woman, aged between 31 and 60 — were arrested in separate operations in Taman Megah Ria and Taman Sri Tebrau. “One of the suspects, a local man, is believed to be the syndicate’s coordinator, while two others were identified as suppliers,” he told reporters at the Johor police contingent headquarters today.

items.title: Ahmad Zahid: Stop blaming government for fuel prices, Malaysia in ‘crisis preparation mode’
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 2 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the country is currently in crisis preparedness mode following global uncertainties which affect fuel prices. Therefore, Ahmad Zahid who is also the minister of rural and regional development, asked all parties not to point fingers at the government regarding the fuel price hike. He said the current situation was caused by the global crisis and required the cooperation of all parties to deal with it together.

items.title: Economy minister: Malaysian Vision Valley Project 60pc complete, targeted to finish this year despite delays
items.summary: NILAI, April 2 — The Malaysian Vision Valley (MVV) project is showing renewed progress after previous delays and is now targeted for completion within the year, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir. After a site visit here today, the minister confirmed that the project, a massive state-led economic growth corridor in Negeri Sembilan, has surpassed 60 per cent completion following the appointment of a rescue contractor. “Taking into account the challenges involved, the target remains to complete the project within this year, with emphasis placed on expediting the works,” he saiid, adding that the project’s cost remains within the existing contract terms.

items.title: We can’t pass on rising costs: Malaysia’s tourism industry pleads for urgent govt aid, subsidies
items.summary: The Malaysian Tourism Federation (MTF) said the Malaysian government needs to urgently help the local tourism industry now, as the tourism operators’ financial burden continues to increase. MTF president Sri Ganesh Michiel explained how Malaysia’s travel industry works, where tour package prices are locked in advance: Malaysia’s travel agencies design travel packages and then negotiate and sell them to travel agencies abroad. Sri Ganesh Michiel, president of Malaysian Tourism Federation, should immediately give out fuel subsidies to tourism transport operators, as the local tourism industry may otherwise be forced to absorb higher costs and bear losses.

items.title: Penang KPDN seizes RM415,877 in subsidised diesel, steps up crackdown on misuse
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, April 2 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) in Penang has recorded eight cases of misappropriated subsidised diesel, with total seizures amounting to RM415,877.95. Penang KPDN director S. Jegan said the cases, recorded between January and April this year, resulted in five arrests. KPDN Penang also received four public complaints related to the misuse of subsidised fuel.

items.title: Azam stays tight-lipped on Bloomberg report: ‘I don’t want to comment’
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 2 — Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki today declined to comment on recent reports by Bloomberg concerning allegations linked to his shareholdings. Bloomberg previously published an investigative report highlighting Azam’s shareholdings in several listed companies, alongside allegations that senior MACC officers had colluded with a group of businessmen in attempts to take control of targeted firms. Azam and the MACC have denied any wrongdoing.

items.title: MACC chief urges public to see beyond ‘bad apples’ in law enforcement agencies
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 2 — Public perception towards enforcement officers must change, Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said today, noting that the actions of a few “bad apples” often overshadow the majority who serve with integrity. Speaking at the Multiagency Taskforce Engagement Session at MACC headquarters here, Azam said negative perceptions frequently arise whenever issues of corruption or misconduct surface, even though many officers continue to carry out their duties professionally. “Enforcement personnel are frequently associated with corruption whenever any issue arises.

items.title: MACC chief: Corporate criminals manipulating shares openly, agencies must keep up
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 2 — Share manipulation and other forms of corporate crime are increasingly being carried out openly, Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said today, stressing the need for authorities to step up efforts to detect such activities. Speaking at the Multiagency Taskforce Engagement Session at the MACC headquarters here, Azam said corporate criminals are using complex structures and professional networks to hide fraudulent activities, including tax evasion, money laundering and manipulation of shareholdings. He said corporate criminals also often rely on networks of professionals such as accountants and lawyers, as well as insiders within relevant agencies, to facilitate illegal activities while reducing the risk of detection.

items.title: Johor Customs bust illegal ketum water factory, seize RM76,796 in leaves and bottled liquid
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 2 — The Johor Customs Department has recently raided an illegal ketum water processing factory in Taman Kempas Utama here with a seizure of RM76,7963 worth of bottles. The house raid netted 103 kilogrammes of ketum leaves and 189.50 litres of ketum water with a total value of RM9,805. Following that, a total of 442.9 kilogrammes of ketum leaves and 1,642.5 litres of ketum water with a total value of RM66,991 was later seized at the factory.

items.title: Anwar admits justice still out of reach for many, vows reforms to close gap
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim acknowledged today that many of Malaysia’s poorest and most marginalised still lack meaningful access to justice, a gap his government aims to bridge. He said that, for this reason, his government has made access to justice a central pillar of its broader reform agenda. He said it bears remembering that the law facilitates the day-to-day lives of citizens, reminding current and upcoming law practitioners to ensure the law is utilised for the public good.

items.title: First South Korean in Malaysia to ace SPM: Kim Na Eun scores 10 A+ including BM
items.summary: MIRI, April 2 — Kim Na Eun, 17, has become the first South Korean student in Malaysia to score A+ in all 10 subjects she sat for in the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) 2025, including Bahasa Malaysia. We did not expect her to get straight A+ in all subjects, including Bahasa Malaysia, as she struggled with learning Bahasa Malaysia during her primary school years,” he said. Na Eun took Bahasa Melayu, English, Moral, History, Mathematics, Additional Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and Bible Knowledge for SPM 2025.

items.title: Nationwide sting: KPDN cracks down on RON95 smugglers in Johor, modified 4WD in Sabah, LPG stockpile in Perak
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 2 — The Johor Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) will intensify enforcement operations to curb cases of smuggling or misappropriation of subsidised RON95 petrol, including undercover operations, especially at 31 petrol stations near the state border. “Monitoring will be enhanced where teams will operate statically and undercover methods, especially at high-risk petrol stations,” she told Bernama. She said this when asked to comment on whether monitoring and enforcement would be intensified to curb cases of smuggling of subsidised RON95 fuel.

items.title: Peak romance: Why Syed Saddiq’s proposal left Bella Astillah breathless at 4,000 metres
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — An exhausting climb up Mount Kinabalu in January culminated in a surprise proposal for actress Bella Astillah, who has now opened up about the emotional moment she accepted Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman’s hand in marriage. Alhamdulillah, I said ‘yes’ within just a few seconds.” She also shared that her two children reacted positively to the news, a conversation initiated by Syed Saddiq himself. “My children responded very positively.

items.title: Police tracking down suspect after anti‑drugs officer injured in knife attack during Bangsar operation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Police are tracking down a local man who acted aggressively and injured an officer of the National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK) with a knife during an operation at Jalan Kerinchi, Bangsar here yesterday. Brickfields district police chief ACP Hoo Chang Hook said the incident occurred at 5.08pm at a car wash premises next to Bangsar South Mall when the 48-year-old AADK officer suffered injuries to his palm and right thumb after being attacked by the suspect. Members of the public with information are urged to contact investigating officer Insp Syed Hamiz Azfar Syed Mohd Najib at the Brickfields district police headquarters or any nearby police station to assist in the investigation.

items.title: Fake news, real trouble: MCMC tracks down five users behind TV3-style fuel price hoax
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has launched an investigation into five individuals suspected of spreading false information on the social media platform Threads about an impending fuel price hike. The viral content, which falsely claimed that petrol and diesel prices would increase, was deemed misleading and capable of causing public anxiety. MCMC noted that the misinformation had been edited to mimic the visual identity of television broadcaster Buletin TV3.

items.title: Teenager drowns at Gunung Ledang Waterfall after slipping during outing with friends
items.summary: Tangkak Fire and Rescue Station operations commander Fazlan Maraman said the victim, Muhammad Zaffran Abu Samah, 15, was pronounced dead by the Ministry of Health staff who arrived at the scene. “Upon arrival, the Fire and Rescue Operations Team (PKO) found that the victim had fallen and drowned while bathing with two friends. He said they received an emergency call regarding the incident at 5.27 pm and a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT) together with an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit, comprising eight personnel from the Tangkak Fire and Rescue Station, were dispatched to the scene.

items.title: Anthony Loke visits grieving widow, offers comfort and support after tragic crash in Klang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke paid a visit to the wife of the motorcyclist who died after being hit by a driver believed to be under the influence of alcohol and drugs in Klang, Selangor, on Sunday. He said he visited the family of the late Amirul Hafiz Omar, 33, yesterday, and was accompanied by Road Transport Department (JPJ) director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli. Loke said he also personally provided financial assistance to the man’s widow, Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, to help lighten the family’s burden.

items.title: Unlicensed, unregistered, unchecked: The Selangor children’s home where boys were allegedly abused for 10 years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Police have arrested the operator of an unlicensed children’s home and three others, uncovering a horrifying decade of alleged sexual abuse that has left multiple young male residents hospitalised with injuries consistent with repeated assault. The home had been operating for approximately 10 years, the New Straits Times reported. Police have arrested four suspects, including the 31-year-old male operator and a 17-year-old resident of the home.

items.title: Johor immigration swoops on luxury apartment, detains Chinese nationals with fake Mexican passport
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 2 — The Johor Immigration Department detained four Chinese nationals for using fake passports and overstaying in the country. Its director, Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus, said the suspects, aged 43 to 57, were detained at a luxury apartment in Bayu Puteri Tuesday. “One of them used a Mexican international passport to enter and stay in the country, while the others used passports from their country of origin,” he said in a statement.

items.title: ‘I have to be both mother and father now’: Widow of Klang drink-driving victim hopes to rebuild life for her three children
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — With her husband gone and three young children depending on her, Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, is returning to her Perak hometown to rebuild her life — weeks after a driver, allegedly high on alcohol and drugs, killed Amirul Hafiz Omar, 32, on March 29. Nor Nadia, also 32, said her immediate focus is to remain strong for her three children — Muhammad Aqif Amsyar, 9, Muhammad Afif Izz Rayqal, 7, and Nur Humaira, 2 — who are still struggling to comprehend their father’s death. She said that she would be moving back to Kampung Repoh in Batu Kurau to be closer to her family for support, the New Straits Times reported.
